headline: Penang Reclamation Project Threatens RM4.8B Fishing Industry
context: The Penang South Reclamation project to create 4,500 acres of new land worth RM70 billion faced renewed opposition in 2026 from 12,000 fishermen who claim it will destroy their RM4.8 billion annual catch livelihoods.

cards.big: RM70 billion reclamation versus RM4.8 billion fishing industry — 12,000 fishermen say they'll lose everything
cards.sub: Environmental Impact Assessment approved despite marine biologists warning of 60% coastal ecosystem loss

cards.big: PSR project creates 300,000 jobs over 20 years but immediately displaces 12,000 fishermen earning RM2,800 monthly
cards.sub: Net economic benefit calculation ignores RM48 billion in fishing revenue lost over the same 20-year period

cards.big: Fishing communities in Teluk Kumbar and Gertak Sanggul have occupied this coastline for over 200 years
cards.sub: Compensation packages averaging RM50,000 per fisherman cover less than 18 months of lost income

cards.big: DOE approved the EIA with 72 conditions but monitoring compliance budget allocated is only RM8 million over 10 years
cards.sub: Previous reclamation projects in Malacca met only 34% of EIA conditions — enforcement is structurally weak

cards.big: Burying a 200-year-old fishing community under reclaimed land for condos isn't development — it's displacement with a business plan
Should major reclamation projects require binding referendums from communities within 10km radius before approval?
